items.question_text: A controller receives notification of a SECNOT. What is the controller's primary action under 9-2-11?
items.explanation: Paragraph 9-2-11 requires the controller to forward all information on the subject aircraft to the OS/CIC. If information is unknown, the controller broadcasts the call sign on all frequencies and advises the OS/CIC of the response.

choices.text: Issue a security NOTAM and suspend all departures until the aircraft is located.
Forward all information on the subject aircraft to the OS/CIC.
Contact law enforcement directly before notifying facility supervision.
Treat the aircraft as NORDO unless it fails to respond to two calls.

items.question_text: Under 9-2-11, what should the controller do if information about the SECNOT subject aircraft is not known?
items.explanation: When the information is not known, 9-2-11 directs the controller to broadcast the call sign on all frequencies and advise the OS/CIC of the response.

choices.text: Wait for the aircraft to check in before taking any action.
Ask only adjacent sectors whether the call sign is on frequency.
Broadcast the call sign on all frequencies and advise the OS/CIC of the response.
Cancel the SECNOT unless the aircraft is radar identified.

items.question_text: Fill in the blank: For a SECNOT, if information is not known, broadcast the _____ on all frequencies.
items.explanation: The paragraph says to broadcast the call sign on all frequencies when information about the subject aircraft is not known.

choices.text: call sign

items.question_text: Which response best captures the operating principle of a SECNOT action in 9-2-11?
items.explanation: The operational point is rapid information flow through the OS/CIC, plus broad frequency checks when the aircraft information is not known.

choices.text: SECNOT handling is mainly a pilot advisory function; ATC action is optional unless the aircraft is in distress.
The controller should keep the event local unless the aircraft enters another facility's airspace.
The controller should forward known subject-aircraft information to the OS/CIC and use all-frequency call-sign broadcasts if needed to locate or confirm a response.
The controller should stop using the aircraft call sign on frequency to avoid alerting the subject aircraft.

items.situation: You receive a SECNOT for an aircraft that may be in your facility's area. You have a partial call sign and no current position.
items.para_context: For a SECNOT, forward subject-aircraft information to the OS/CIC; if information is not known, broadcast the call sign on all frequencies and advise the OS/CIC of the response.
items.instruction: Select the action that best follows 9-2-11.
items.explanation: With incomplete information, 9-2-11 still requires the controller to involve the OS/CIC and broadcast the call sign on all frequencies, then advise the OS/CIC of the response.

choices.text: Broadcast the call sign on all frequencies and advise the OS/CIC of any response.
Wait until radar identification is established before notifying the OS/CIC.
Forward the partial call sign to law enforcement only; do not use ATC frequencies.
Transmit only on the last known frequency because all-frequency broadcasts are reserved for emergencies.

items.situation: During a security notice event, another controller says, 'We do not know enough yet. Do not bother the OS/CIC until the aircraft answers.'
items.instruction: Use the source rule to decide whether that advice is correct.
items.explanation: The advice is wrong. The paragraph requires forwarding all known information to the OS/CIC, and when information is not known, broadcasting the call sign on all frequencies and advising the OS/CIC of the response.

choices.text: Correct; OS/CIC notification waits until the aircraft answers.
Incorrect; forward known information to the OS/CIC and broadcast the call sign on all frequencies if information is not known.
Correct only if the aircraft is outside controlled airspace.
Incorrect, but the only required action is to notify adjacent sectors.
